title: Upper Respiratory Tract Infection: The Common Cold
license_notes: Government antimicrobial stewardship guidance. Use for evidence support and antibiotic stewardship language, not as copy/paste discharge text.
supports: Most upper respiratory infections are viral.
Antibiotics are not recommended for uncomplicated common cold.
Symptom care can include analgesics, antipyretics, and selected over-the-counter therapies when appropriate.
Escalation symptoms include difficulty breathing, confusion, high fever, or symptoms not improving as expected.
